exemplar.title: To what extent are Acetylcholinesterase inhibitors effective in minimizing cognitive decline by slowing the progression of Alzheimerâs disease in older adults?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent are Acetylcholinesterase inhibitors effective in minimizing cognitive decline by slowing the progression of Alzheimerâs disease in older adults?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear and appropriate purpose and focus.
Research question is clearly stated, focused, and effectively connected to the discussion.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks a transparent, systematic search strategy and explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria.
Methodology details are not presented in a distinct section, reducing clarity of the research process.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate demonstrates an excellent grasp of focus and purpose (two strands at the 5â6 band) but falls short of a fully rigorous methodology (a 3â4 band performance). Averaging these performances yields a band-5 overall score out of 6.

strengths: Accurate use of subject-specific terminology such as âdouble-blind placebo-controlledâ and âneuroplasticityâ in appropriate contexts.
Solid understanding of Alzheimerâs neuropathology and pharmacodynamics, as shown in detailed explanations of assessment tools (MMSE, ADAS-Cog).
weaknesses: Occasional factual imprecision (e.g., confusing inhibition versus enhancement of cholinergic responses).
Insufficient reporting of statistical significance (p-values, confidence intervals) and missing in-text citations for key statistics.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work displays generally good knowledge and use of terminology (3â4 band) but contains enough lapses in precision and source application to warrant a mid-band score of 3 out of 6 rather than a higher band.

strengths: Discussion weighs methodological strengths against key limitations and considers practical implications (costs, side-effects).
Literature selection is mostly relevant, covering clinical trials, economic data, and comparative approaches.
weaknesses: Analysis stops short of integrating findings into a coherent comparative framework or quantifying effect sizes.
Some critiques (e.g., open-label bias) are noted without fully connecting them to the research questionâs validity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands sit solidly in the 4â6 band (research relevance 5, analysis depth 5) while the third is in the 7â9 band (evaluation 7). The average performance aligns with a mid-band score of 5 out of 12, reflecting effective but not fully comprehensive critical thinking.

strengths: Clearly appropriate structure with all required sections and logical headings.
Consistent layout conventions, including page numbers and an alphabetized reference list.
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in in-text citation formatting.
Analytical discussion and descriptive context are sometimes interwoven, reducing reader clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the criteria for the 3â4 band overall, with only minor layout and structural issues. A score of 3 out of 4 reflects solid adherence to conventions with room for tighter organization.

strandSelections.reasoning: Subject-specific terminology such as âdouble-blind placebo-controlledâ, âneuroplasticityâ, âbutyrylcholinesteraseâ and âexternal validityâ is used mostly correctly and in appropriate contexts.  Minor lapses (e.g., confusing âinhibitsâ with âenhancesâ cholinergic responses) prevent full consistency, but overall precision is above basic competence, fitting the 3-4 band for terminology and conceptual use.

The document indicates page numbers, hierarchical headings, consistent font style and an alphabetised reference list in a recognised citation style.  Minor inconsistencies (e.g., mixed in-text citation formats) are present but layout conventions are largely met, warranting placement in the 3-4 band.

The student shows solid understanding of Alzheimerâs neuropathology (tau tangles, Î²-amyloid), pharmacodynamics of AChEIs, and assessment tools (MMSE, ADAS-Cog).  Empirical findings from major trials are summarised accurately and applied to the argument.  Some explanations remain surface-level (e.g., memantineâs mechanism) and occasional factual imprecision appears, so the work sits comfortably in the âgood knowledgeâ range (3-4 band) rather than the consistently excellent range.

The essay employs a largely secondary-research methodology: a comparative literature review of eight peer-reviewed studies plus economic and epidemiological sources.  The sources are mostly relevant and the student justifies their inclusion (e.g., double-blind trials, large samples).  However, criteria for selecting or excluding studies are not systematically explained, and there is limited discussion of search strategies or data-handling procedures.  This indicates a âmostly completeâ but not fully rigorous methodology, aligning with the 3-4 band.

The student states a precise research topicâevaluating the effectiveness of acetylcholinesterase inhibitors in slowing Alzheimerâs-related cognitive declineâand provides a concise background that explains why this issue is academically and socially important. Key terms (e.g., cognitive decline, acetylcholinesterase) are defined in context, and the purpose of comparing the drugs with alternative treatments is explicit.  Overall, the topic is communicated accurately and purposefully, fulfilling the descriptor for the 5-6 band.

The literature selected (clinical trials, systematic reviews, animal studies) is generally relevant to the question and demonstrates an adequate range across drug and non-drug approaches.  Nevertheless, gaps remain (e.g., no coverage of recent large-scale meta-analyses) and some sources are cited only descriptively.  This places the research base solidly within the 4-6 âadequateâ band rather than the higher âmostly relevantâ 7-9 band.

The research question â âTo what extent are acetylcholinesterase inhibitors effective in minimizing cognitive decline by slowing the progression of Alzheimerâs disease in older adults?â â is clearly worded, focused and tightly linked to the ensuing discussion of donepezil, galantamine and rivastigmine.  Its scope (older adults, cognitive measures, comparison with alternatives) is sufficiently narrow to be addressed within an EE, demonstrating effective focus in line with the 5-6 band.

The essay follows the prescribed EE structure: title page with word count, table of contents, introduction, body sections, discussion, conclusion and works cited.  Headings and sub-headings guide the reader logically and all mandatory components appear present, satisfying the criteria for a clearly appropriate structure (3-4 band).

The discussion section weighs strengths (double-blind design, large samples) against limitations (sampling bias, short study durations, animal-human generalisation) and considers practical implications (cost, side-effects).  Arguments are generally logical and structured, showing a moderately critical stance rather than mere description.  The evaluation is clear but sometimes cursory, so it meets the âreasoned argument with partially critical evaluationâ descriptor at the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The student summarises outcome data (mean score changes, duration effects) and attempts cross-study comparisons.  There is some evaluation of methodological rigour (e.g., open-label bias, ecological validity), but the analysis stops short of integrating findings into a coherent comparative model or quantifying effect sizes.  Conclusions are partially supported, reflecting clear but not fully developed analysis â characteristic of the 4-6 band.

annotations.comment: The detailed explanation of MMSE, ADAS-Cog, and CIBIC-plus assessments, including scoring ranges, shows strong understanding of empirical measurement tools.
The phrase ânon-lethal placeboâ is misleading; use âinactive controlâ or âplaceboâ to accurately reflect standard experimental terminology.
The brief note on open-label bias lacks connection to its impact on study relevance; integrate this critique to strengthen analytical depth.
Including rivastigmine results that contradict prior findings demonstrates commendable critical evaluation of diverse evidence.
Interpretation of CIBIC-plus scores needs clarification on statistical or clinical significance; specify effect thresholds to guide reader understanding.
Identifying the lack of a double-blind design in the Memantine study shows strong methodological critique and supports evaluation of validity.
The Memantine study description omits details on randomization and control procedures; precise methodology is needed for full source application.
The discussion of neuroplasticity and neurogenesis in exercise interventions reflects interdisciplinary knowledge and enriches the argument.
The analogy to patient H.M. lacks explanation of its translational relevance to Alzheimerâs; elaborate on how this case study informs human treatment.
The references are alphabetized and comprehensive, demonstrating well-organized scholarly presentation and adherence to academic conventions.
Including the word count at the start meets the formatting requirement by transparently indicating adherence to the 4â000-word limit.
The title appears as plain text rather than a formal, centered title on a title page; consider creating a dedicated title page with the investigation title and candidate details.
The table of contents clearly outlines all sections and corresponding page ranges, providing a coherent roadmap for the reader.
The dotted leaders in the table of contents are inconsistently spaced; standardize leader dots or tab stops to improve readability and professional appearance.

The research question is stated clearly and precisely, with a focused scope on acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, cognitive decline, and older adults, effectively guiding the ensuing analysis and fulfilling the criterion for a wellâfocused question.
The introduction lacks an explicit methodology section outlining literature search strategy, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and source selection rationale.
Methodology details are embedded in the narrative without a distinct âMethodologyâ header; include a clear methods section to enhance organization.
The research aims and planned studies are clearly articulated, demonstrating effective focus and a coherent method for addressing the research question.
The literature review section omits description of search databases and keyword strategies; specify these to provide transparent methodology.
In-text citation âRoggers and Sharon 1998â deviates from standard style; adopt a consistent MLA or APA parenthetical format throughout.
Reporting precise mean changes in ADAS-Cog, CIBIC-plus, and MMSE scores demonstrates strong data application and clear presentation of empirical findings.
Results omit p-values or confidence intervals; inclusion of statistical significance metrics is necessary to support claims rigorously.
The detailed description of Galantamine dosage escalation protocols exhibits strong understanding of pharmacological study design.
The background demonstrates solid subject knowledge of Alzheimerâs neuropathology, including tau tangles, amyloid plaques, and neurotransmitter reduction, applied accurately in context.
The statistic âaffects 6% of the aging populationâ is unsupported by an inline citation; include precise in-text referencing to reinforce source credibility.
Contextualizing economic costs and social impacts of Alzheimerâs treatment strengthens the relevance of the research focus and enriches critical evaluation.
Background information and evaluative critique are interwoven; separate descriptive context from analytical discussion to improve structure and reader clarity.
Citation formats vary in italics, punctuation, and URL presentation; apply a single style guide consistently across all references.
